component_role_description: A panel is a decorative UI CONTAINER — a stretchable frame that holds other content (text, other widgets) at runtime. Its defining feature is a 9-slice structure: thick, symmetric decorative borders at the edges with a simple, plain-or-repeating interior that the engine stretches to fit variable content sizes. A panel is NOT a standalone whole object (like a button is). It is a FRAME whose middle is meant to be filled in, not illustrated.

reference_image.critical_note: If this image is a panel, reproduce its style. If it's a different component type (kit mode), use it only for art-style cues and invent a panel-shaped frame from scratch.

output_constraints.interior_fill: simple, uniform, or small-scale repeating pattern only — absolutely NO detailed content, illustrations, or focal art in the middle third
output_constraints.symmetric_corners: all four corners must be visually consistent (top-left pairs with top-right, etc.)
output_constraints.edge_symmetry: top and bottom edges mirror; left and right edges mirror
output_constraints.border_thickness: borders should occupy at least 15% of the shorter image dimension so the 9-slice has room to work

shape_profile.description: A panel is a decorative rectangular container frame with a 9-slice-compatible structure: thick symmetric borders around a simple interior. It is NOT a standalone whole object — it is a frame whose middle will be stretched to hold other content.
shape_profile.typical_aspect_ratio: 1:1 to 4:3 (squarish to slightly wider than tall)
required_visual_features: thick symmetric decorative borders (same treatment on all four edges; corners that visually pair up)
a simple, plain, or small-scale repeating interior — no focal artwork in the middle
9-slice compatibility: the border corners + edges should be the detailed parts, not the centre
must_not_look_like: a button (buttons are whole pressable objects with label areas; panels are empty containers)
an icon or illustration with a scene in the middle
a progress bar (panels are substantially taller than progress bars)
a single solid rectangle with no visible border treatment

normal.description: Canonical resting panel — a decorative rectangular frame with thick symmetric borders and a plain or gently-textured interior. No state-specific modifications.

forbidden_content: a detailed scene, illustration, character, or focal artwork in the middle of the panel
asymmetric corners (top-left must pair with top-right; top pair must pair with bottom pair)
thin or missing borders — this is a frame, so borders must be substantial
baked-in text, labels, or UI elements inside the panel (panels host text at runtime; they don't contain it)
a button-shaped or icon-shaped silhouette — panels are containers, not whole pressable/clickable units
stripping or altering any backgrounds, frames, or textures that are visible in the source
priority_rules: A panel is a FRAME, not a whole object. The middle MUST be simple enough to stretch without revealing illustration.
9-slice structure is non-negotiable: thick symmetric corners + stretchable edges + plain center.
DIMENSIONS must match the reference image exactly.
Style (colours, line weight, edge treatment) comes from the reference; the SHAPE must be a rectangular panel frame.
Symmetry matters more than creative detail here — a panel's job is to tile cleanly.
